creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Spun off from bug 255898 comment 1.

Within the current edition of the file: 

> # $FreeBSD: docs/README.TXT 117 2013-01-13 22:58:33Z gjb $

> … non-x86 architecture builds are cross-built, not natively generated on 
> their respective platform. …

Is that still entirely true?

> … Snapshots are expected to be generated weekly. …

Something from around the time of bug 255898 might have made me think about a change to this wording (for expectations to be realistic). Whatever that thought was, it might be negated by the document's mention of freebsd-snapshots@ (where, for example, I see <https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-snapshots/2022-July/000116.html> an exception to the norm).

> FreeBSD-{BRANCH}-{ARCH}-{DATE}-{SVNREV}

– that, and other mentions of svn/Subversion are outdated. 

> … CHECKSUM.MD5 and CHECKSUM.SHA256 …

Instead: "… CHECKSUM.SHA256 and CHECKSUM.SHA512 …

> … Be sure to check the mailing lists for … (e.g., freebsd-sparc64 …

Re: <https://www.freebsd.org/platforms/> with sparc64 largely no longer supported, a different platform will make a better example.
